title: GripMessageAck
description: An acknowledgement of a GripMessage by its `id`. ONLY useful when the underlying transport doesn't already track delivery — most modern transports (TCP, WebSocket, HTTP, MQTT QoS levels, etc.) handle ack natively, and producers should rely on those mechanisms first. Ship this schema when you're moving messages over a fire-and-forget channel (e.g. simplex radio with no return path until the next handshake, or a one-way satellite link) and need delivery state to live in the protocol payload itself.

extensions.description: App-specific namespaced data. Keys are app slugs (kebab-case recommended); values are objects owned by that app.

address.description: A structured participant address. Same shape as `address` in message.schema.json — duplicated here so each schema is self-contained.

schemaVersion.description: Version of the message-ack schema this document conforms to.

ackOf.description: The `id` of the message being acknowledged. Must match the original message's `id` field exactly — implementations correlate by string equality.

from.description: The acknowledging participant — typically the original recipient (or the recipient's transport agent on its behalf).

status.description: Delivery state. 'delivered' = the message reached the recipient's device. 'read' = the recipient processed / displayed it (e.g. the crew saw the broadcast). 'failed' = the recipient couldn't process the message; see `reason` for context.

reason.description: Optional human-readable explanation. Primarily useful when status = 'failed' (e.g. 'unknown carNumber', 'message body too long for radio').

priority.description: Optional transport-layer priority hint. Repeaters and low-bandwidth networks can use this to prioritize critical traffic (bump transmit power, allocate bandwidth, skip the routine queue). Should typically mirror the priority of the acked message.

clientCapturedAt.description: ISO 8601 UTC instant the ack was authored on the producing device.

serverReceivedAt.description: ISO 8601 UTC instant the ack was received by the routing server. Producers should leave this null.
